text_original: DSM is used to assess and diagnose  mental disorders. For more information on DMDD,  visit www.nimh.nih.gov/dmdd.  What is the difference between  typical and severe irritability?  All children can become irritable sometimes.  It’s a normal reaction to frustration. Children  experiencing severe irritability (as observed in  DMDD) have difficulty tolerating frustration and  have outbursts that are out of proportion to the  situation at hand. These outbursts occur more  often and are more severe than expected for  children their age.  For example, a parent tells the child to stop  playing a game
text_simplified: اضطراب خلل المزاج التخريبي اضطراب مزاجي شديد قد يعطّل حياة المصاب به؛ تظهر فيه نوبات غضب حادة لفظية أو سلوكية — كالعدوان تجاه الأشخاص أو الممتلكات — غير متناسبة مع أدنى المثيرات. يحتاج تقييماً وعلاجاً متخصصاً.
title: نوبات الغضب الشديدة في اضطراب خلل المزاج التخريبي
